ddls:
CREATE TABLE "counties" (
  "county_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each county */,
  "county_name" TEXT /* Name of the county */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population of the county */,
  "area" REAL /* Area of the county in square kilometers */,
  "density" REAL /* Population density of the county */,
  "median_income" INTEGER /* Median income of the county */,
  "number_of_roadways" INTEGER /* Number of roadways in the county */,
  "total_road_length" REAL /* Total length of roadways in the county */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("county_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "cities" (
  "city_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each city */,
  "city_name" TEXT /* Name of the city */,
  "county_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the county where the city is located */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population of the city */,
  "area" REAL /* Area of the city in square kilometers */,
  "density" REAL /* Population density of the city */,
  "median_income" INTEGER /* Median income of the city */,
  "number_of_roadways" INTEGER /* Number of roadways in the city */,
  "total_road_length" REAL /* Total length of roadways in the city */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("city_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "vehicles" (
  "vehicle_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each vehicle record */,
  "vehicle_type" TEXT /* Type of vehicle involved in the incident */,
  "incident_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the incident this vehicle is involved in */,
  "make" TEXT /* Make of the vehicle */,
  "model" TEXT /* Model of the vehicle */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year of the vehicle */,
  "color" TEXT /* Color of the vehicle */,
  "license_plate" TEXT /* License plate number of the vehicle */,
  "driver_id" INTEGER /* ID of the driver of the vehicle */,
  "driver_age" INTEGER /* Age of the driver */,
  "driver_gender" TEXT /* Gender of the driver */,
  "driver_license_state" TEXT /* State where the driver's license is issued */,
  "driver_experience" INTEGER /* Years of driving experience of the driver */,
  "vehicle_damage" TEXT /* Extent of damage to the vehicle */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("vehicle_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "road_conditions" (
  "condition_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each road condition type */,
  "condition_description" TEXT /* Description of the road condition */,
  "surface_temperature" REAL /* Surface temperature of the road at the time of the incident */,
  "road_moisture" TEXT /* Moisture level of the road */,
  "road_defects" TEXT /* Any defects on the road */,
  "road_visibility" TEXT /* Visibility on the road */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("condition_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "weather_conditions" (
  "weather_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weather condition */,
  "weather_description" TEXT /* Description of the weather condition */,
  "temperature" REAL /* Temperature at the time of the incident */,
  "humidity" INTEGER /* Humidity level at the time of the incident */,
  "wind_speed" REAL /* Wind speed at the time of the incident */,
  "wind_direction" TEXT /* Wind direction at the time of the incident */,
  "precipitation" TEXT /* Type and amount of precipitation */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("weather_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last login */,
  "access_level" TEXT /* Access level of the user */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the user account */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "incident_access_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access log entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Identifier for the user accessing the data */,
  "incident_id" INTEGER /* Identifier for the incident being accessed */,
  "access_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the access occurred */,
  "action" TEXT /* Action performed by the user */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the user's browser */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the access */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_incident_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
